info.title: APIFlash Screenshot API
info.description: APIFlash provides a website screenshot API that renders screenshots using real Chrome browsers in the cloud. Supports full page capture, element selection, custom CSS/JS injection, geolocation spoofing, and various output formats. All screenshots are generated server-side using Chromium.

tags.name: Screenshots
tags.description: Website screenshot capture and quota management

get.description: Takes a screenshot of a website URL and returns it as an image or a JSON response with the image URL. Supports numerous options for controlling viewport size, format, quality, wait conditions, and more.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your APIFlash access key

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL of the website to capture

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The image format of the captured screenshot

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: The width of the browser viewport in pixels

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: The height of the browser viewport in pixels

parameters.name: full_page
parameters.description: Whether to capture a full-page screenshot of the entire page

parameters.name: quality
parameters.description: The quality of the image between 0 and 100. Only applies to jpeg and webp format

parameters.name: delay
parameters.description: The delay in seconds before taking the screenshot after the page has loaded (max 10 seconds)

parameters.name: css
parameters.description: A CSS string to inject in the page before capturing the screenshot

parameters.name: js
parameters.description: A JavaScript string to execute in the page before capturing the screenshot

parameters.name: scroll_page
parameters.description: Whether to scroll the page down before capturing the screenshot (triggers lazy-loaded images)

parameters.name: fresh
parameters.description: Whether to always generate a fresh screenshot (no cache). Uses 1 extra credit when using TTL caching.

parameters.name: response_type
parameters.description: Response type. 'image' returns the raw image. 'json' returns a JSON response with the image URL.

parameters.name: no_cookie_banners
parameters.description: Whether to remove cookie banners from the page before capturing the screenshot

parameters.name: no_ads
parameters.description: Whether to block ads on the page before capturing the screenshot

parameters.name: no_tracking
parameters.description: Whether to block tracking scripts on the page

parameters.name: element
parameters.description: A CSS selector of the element to capture instead of the full page

parameters.name: transparent
parameters.description: Whether to make the background transparent. Only works with png format.

parameters.name: wait_until
parameters.description: The event to wait for before capturing the screenshot

parameters.name: time_zone
parameters.description: The timezone to emulate (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London)

parameters.name: fail_on_status
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of HTTP status codes that should cause the API to return an error. Use '2xx,3xx,4xx,5xx' format.

parameters.name: extract_html
parameters.description: Whether to extract and return the page HTML instead of a screenshot

parameters.name: extract_text
parameters.description: Whether to extract and return the page text content instead of a screenshot

parameters.name: thumbnail_width
parameters.description: Width of the generated thumbnail in pixels. The image will be resized to this width.

parameters.name: accept_language
parameters.description: Set the Accept-Language header for the request (e.g., en-US,en;q=0.5)

parameters.name: user_agent
parameters.description: Set a custom user agent string for the request

parameters.name: latitude
parameters.description: Latitude for geolocation spoofing

parameters.name: longitude
parameters.description: Longitude for geolocation spoofing

parameters.name: accuracy
parameters.description: Accuracy in meters for geolocation spoofing

parameters.name: proxy
parameters.description: Proxy URL to use for the request (e.g., http://user:pass@host:port)

parameters.name: ttl
parameters.description: Time to live in seconds for caching the screenshot. Use 0 for no caching.

parameters.name: wait_for
parameters.description: A CSS selector of an element to wait for before capturing the screenshot

parameters.name: no_background
parameters.description: Whether to hide the default white background and allow capturing with transparency

parameters.name: s3_access_key
parameters.description: S3 access key ID for saving the screenshot directly to your S3 bucket

parameters.name: s3_secret_key
parameters.description: S3 secret access key for saving the screenshot directly to your S3 bucket

parameters.name: s3_bucket
parameters.description: S3 bucket name for saving the screenshot

parameters.name: s3_key
parameters.description: S3 object key (path) for the screenshot

parameters.name: s3_endpoint
parameters.description: Custom S3 endpoint URL (for S3-compatible services like DigitalOcean Spaces, Backblaze B2)

parameters.name: s3_region
parameters.description: S3 region for the bucket

200.description: Screenshot captured successfully. Returns image data or JSON depending on response_type parameter.

get.description: Returns the remaining screenshot quota for the current billing period.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your APIFlash access key

accessKey.name: access_key
accessKey.description: API access key passed as a query parameter

url.description: URL of the generated screenshot image

limit.description: Total screenshot quota for the current billing period

remaining.description: Remaining screenshots in the current billing period

reset.description: Unix timestamp when the quota resets
